programme.title: Knock at the Cabin
programme.desc: M Night Shyamalan's jarringly intimate end-of-days horror with Dave Bautista. An armed group insist that a family of three must make an impossible life-or-death choice.

programme.title: Other People's Children
programme.desc: Affecting drama with Virginie Efira as a single woman in her late 30s who wants children, and is falling in love with a man and his four-year-old daughter. In French/subs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: The Heiress
programme.desc: Oscar-winner Olivia de Havilland is joined by Montgomery Clift and Ralph Richardson in William Wyler's dark psychological romance, adapted from Henry James's novel Washington Square.

programme.title: Anzio
programme.desc: Epic Second World War drama starring Robert Mitchum. A reporter gets trapped behind enemy lines during the costly 1944 Allied invasion of Italy. With Peter Falk and Robert Ryan.

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora!
programme.desc: Oscar-winning drama portraying the bombing of Pearl Harbor, from both American and Japanese perspectives. With Joseph Cotten and So Yamamura.

programme.title: A Knight's Tale
programme.desc: Modern swashbuckling medieval action adventure starring Heath Ledger, Shannyn Sossamon and Paul Bettany. A lowly squire jousts his way to the top, with help from Geoffrey Chaucer.

programme.title: Good Luck to You, Leo Grande
programme.desc: Drama starring Emma Thompson as a recently widowed, retired teacher who has a sexual bucket list she wants to get around to now that she has the time and, perhaps, even the energy.

programme.title: Widows
programme.desc: Steve McQueen's thriller follows the widows of some serious criminals as they mount a daring heist. The star-studded cast includes Viola Davis, Colin Farrell and Michelle Rodriguez.

programme.title: On the Come Up
programme.desc: Jamila Gray stars as Lil' Law- a struggling young rapper who wants to make her fortune to help her family in director Sanaa Lathan's hip-hop-battling-coming-of-fame drama.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: Western starring James Stewart. A gold prospector, tired of reprisal killings between whites and Native Americans, offers a truce to the Apaches' leader. But tensions resurface.

programme.title: Gun Fury
programme.desc: Western. Rock Hudson stars as a California-bound settler who must ride 1500 miles to the Mexican border to rescue his fiancée (Donna Reed) after she's abducted by an outlaw.

programme.title: Siege of the Saxons
programme.desc: Dark ages adventure. King Arthur is murdered and usurped by his champion knight (Ronald Howard), so an outlaw (Ronald Lewis) seeks the mystic Merlin (John Laurie) to right the wrong.

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: Drama. Elizabeth Taylor plays the Egyptian queen who, to protect her realm, uses political guile and her body to woo Julius Caesar (Rex Harrison) and Mark Antony (Richard Burton).

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: Luc Besson's action-thriller about a young Russian assassin, with Sasha Luss. A scout offers Anna an opportunity to become a model in Paris, but can she escape her links to the KGB.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Partially animated action film based on a popular manga comic. Alita, a deadly cyborg brought back to life by a mourning scientist, sets out to discover her true identity.

programme.title: Sheroes
programme.desc: She-heroes: action-with-attitude. Three young women transform into a Generation Z version of Charlie's Angels to rescue their mate from a drug lord in Thailand. Cue guns and bikinis.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.
